**Prediction and perception: Insights for (and from) tinnitus.** — Neurosci Biobehav Rev (2019). DOI: 10.1016/j.neubiorev.2019.04.008. PMID: 30998951. More than 150 years have passed since Helmholtz first described perception as a process of unconscious inference about the causes of sensations. His ideas have since inspired a wealth of literature investigating the mechanisms underlying these inferences. In recent years, much of this work has converged on the notion that the brain is a hierarchical generative model of its environment that predicts sensations and updates itself based on prediction errors.
